actions.help: Video input source, defaults to webcam
Save annotated video. Omit a value to use Outputs/Video/[stem]_Video_Output.mp4, or supply a custom path.
Save post-run summary CSV. Omit a value to use Outputs/CSV Files/[stem]_summary.csv, or supply a custom path.
Save per-participant scene gaze heatmaps. Omit a value to use Outputs/heatmaps/[stem]_Heatmap_Output (one PNG per participant), or supply a custom directory/prefix path.
Generate post-run time-series charts for each phenomena tracker. Omit a value to use Outputs/Charts/[stem]_Charts.png, or supply a custom path.
Load pipeline configuration from a YAML file. CLI flags override YAML values.
Run in project mode: process all staged videos (DIR/Inputs/Runs/ run folders, or the legacy flat DIR/Inputs/Videos/) using DIR/Pipeline/pipeline.yaml as config.
Project mode: reprocess every video, ignoring the resume ledger. Does not archive prior outputs. Default resumes -- skipping videos whose ledger entry is done with an unchanged config.

Print the project readiness report (requires --project) and exit. Checks pipeline config, weights, VP file, runs, participants/conditions, device, disk, and plugin load errors. Exit 0 if no failures, else 1.
Comma-separated participant labels for single-video mode. Positional: first label maps to track 0, second to track 1, etc. E.g. --participant-ids S70,S71,S72
Path to a participant_ids.csv mapping video filenames to custom participant labels (see docs for format).

Auxiliary video stream. Format: SOURCE:VIDEO_TYPE:LABEL:PID1,PID2 where SOURCE is the file path, VIDEO_TYPE is one of eye_only/face_closeup/wide_closeup/custom, LABEL is a user-defined stream label, and PIDS is a comma-separated list of participant labels. Repeatable for multiple streams.
Enable automatic face detection on wide/face auxiliary streams (default: enabled).
Compute device for all backends: auto, cpu, cuda, or mps.  'auto' selects CUDA > MPS > CPU  (default: auto).
Anonymize faces in the output video: 'blur' applies heavy Gaussian blur, 'black' fills with a solid rectangle. Bare --anonymize means 'blur'.
Fraction of face bbox size added as padding for anonymization (default: 0.3).
Enable bundled performance optimizations: skip phenomena on non-detection frames, throttle dashboard bridge, reduce GUI poll rate.
Run phenomena trackers only every N frames (0 = every frame). Independent of --skip-frames.  (default: 0)
Minimal overlay: disable cone rendering, convergence markers, dwell arcs, and debug text.  Keeps gaze arrows, boxes, badges.
Skip dashboard composition for maximum throughput. Displays the raw annotated frame only.
Print per-stage timing breakdown every 100 frames.
Reuse the previous gaze estimate when a face crop is visually unchanged (mean-abs 32x32 grayscale diff below EPS). 0 disables (default: 0.0). Saves per-face model calls on static footage.
Enable monocular depth estimation.
Explicitly disable depth estimation.
Depth model backend (default: midas_small).
Depth model input resolution (default: 384).
Run depth every N detection cycles (default: 1).
Enable depth-weighted snap scoring.
Depth match weight in snap scoring (default: 0.4).
Half-size of patch for depth sampling (default: 2).
Object Detection Model, defaults to yolov8n.pt
RetinaFace face-detection confidence threshold (default: 0.5, the library default). Lower to catch small/distant faces.
RetinaFace square input resolution in pixels (default: 640, the library default). Raise for small/distant faces at a speed cost.
Object detection confidence threshold, defaults to 0.35
Specified YOLO Object Detection Classes Prompt
Specified YOLO Object Detection Classes Blacklist
Frames between object detection. Higher = faster but less accurate. (Defaults to 1, i.e. process every frame)
Detection scale for Object Recognition
Path to visual prompt file for use with YOLOE object detection models
YOLOE model to use alongside visual prompting for object detection
Run without any object-detection model: faces, gaze rays, and gaze-tip phenomena only (no object hits or lock-on). Not compatible with --vp-file.
Dead-reckon object bboxes for N frames after they disappear (default 0).
Merge or filter overlapping same-class detections.
Overlap strategy: 'filter' keeps highest-conf box, 'merge' creates encompassing box, 'dynamic' chooses per-cluster based on confidence and size (default: dynamic).
Overlap threshold to trigger merge (default: 0.7).
Gaze ray-length multiplier, default 1.0
Dynamically adjust gaze ray-length based on gaze confidence value
Adds circular bounding-box to tip of gaze-rays, used to determine intersection between gaze-rays. Set radius with --tip-radius (default 80).
Pixel radius for --gaze-tips (default 80)
Adaptive ray mode: 'off' disables, 'extend' freely extends the ray toward the nearest object, 'snap' locks the endpoint to the object centre (default: off).
Fraction of bbox half-diagonal added to snap radius (default 0.0)
Snap scoring weight for normalized distance penalty (default 1.0)
Snap scoring weight for angular deviation penalty (default 0.8)
Snap scoring weight for object size reward (default 0.0)
Snap scoring bonus for ray-bbox intersection (default 0.5)
Snap scoring bonus for previous-frame target stickiness (default 0.3)
Hard angular cutoff in degrees: objects beyond this angle from the blended gaze+head direction are never snap candidates (default 60.0).
Blend factor for angular scoring: 0=pure gaze direction, 1=pure head orientation (default 0.3).
Maximum score to accept a snap match. Higher values are more permissive. Set lower to reject poor matches (default 0.8).
Tip-snap distance threshold. -1 = use --snap-dist (default -1).
Tip-snap quality threshold. -1 = use --snap-quality-thresh (default -1).
Minimum per-face gaze confidence for ray-object hit detection. 0.0 = disabled (default).
Extend gaze-object detection N pixels past the visual ray/cone endpoint. 0 = detection matches visual exactly (default: %(default)s).
Scope for --detect-extend: 'objects' extends only ray-object hit detection, 'phenomena' extends only phenomena tracking (mutual gaze, social ref), 'both' extends both (default: objects).
Replaces standard gaze vectors with vision cones of a specified angle in degrees (disabled by default).
Enable fixation lock-on (default: off).
Frames of no-match before releasing the held snap target (default 5).
Frames of consistent match required before engaging snap for the first time. 0 = instant engage (default 0).
Seconds a lost face track stays in the re-ID buffer (default 1.0).
Pitch/yaw angles below this (degrees) are treated as looking forward at the camera. Set to 0 to disable (default 5.0).

Smooth snap mode: smoothly interpolate the ray toward snap targets instead of jumping instantly. 'objects' = smooth object snaps only, 'gaze_tips' = smooth gaze-tip snaps only, 'all' = both (default: off).
EMA rate for smooth snap: lower = smoother/slower, higher = faster/more responsive (default: 0.20).
Path to a Gaze-LLE checkpoint (.pt) for Gazelle blend ray forming. Used alongside a pitch/yaw backend (MGaze, etc.) to periodically correct rays with Gaze-LLE heatmaps.
Gaze-LLE model variant for ray forming (default: gazelle_dinov2_vitb14).
Run the blend-path Gaze-LLE model in half precision (CUDA/MPS only; ignored on CPU). Faster per call; output is NOT byte-identical to fp32, so keep it off for regression baselines.
torch.compile the blend-path Gaze-LLE model (PyTorch 2+, experimental -- MPS support immature). First call pays a warmup cost.
(Legacy) alias for --min-call-gap. If both are given, --min-call-gap wins. Kept so old scripts/YAMLs keep working (default: unset -> min_call_gap default).
Minimum frames between Gaze-LLE inference calls. The scheduler also requires at least one participant to be fixating before firing (default: 30).
Smoothed pitch/yaw velocity (rad/frame) at which a face is treated as 50%% fixating. Lower = safer anchoring (default: 0.04).
Windowed pitch/yaw dispersion (rad) at which a face is treated as 50%% fixating (default: 0.15).
Direction One Euro smoother floor cutoff (Hz). Lower = smoother at rest (default: 1.0).
Direction One Euro responsiveness. Higher = snaps to fast motion (default: 0.5).
Length One Euro smoother floor cutoff (Hz) (default: 1.0).
Length One Euro responsiveness. Lower than direction by default so length holds steadier (default: 0.3).

Seconds the Gaze-LLE-derived ray length persists after an accepted inference before decaying back to the pitch/yaw baseline. Direction reverts quickly on its own; raise this to hold ray reach longer between inferences (default: 5.0).
Use depth map to scale ray length based on scene geometry (default: off).
Ray length multiplier at depth=0 (nearest) (default: 0.5).
Ray length multiplier at depth=1 (farthest) (default: 3.0).
How much depth agreement boosts Gaze-LLE heatmap confidence in the belief update (default: 0.0).
Enable joint-attention tracking.
Temporal consistency window (frames). 0 = disabled (default).
Fraction of faces required for joint attention (default 1.0).
Also detect leadership via gaze-tip convergence (requires --gaze-tips).
Lookback frames for tip-arrival priority (default: 15).
Track per-participant per-object average attention span (mean completed-glance duration). Most salient object shown in HUD.
Enable all gaze-phenomena trackers at once.
Path to a Gazelle checkpoint (.pt).  Activates the Gazelle backend.
Gazelle model variant  (choices: gazelle_dinov2_vitb14, gazelle_dinov2_vitb14_inout, gazelle_dinov2_vitl14, gazelle_dinov2_vitl14_inout;  default: gazelle_dinov2_vitb14).
In/out-of-view confidence threshold for *_inout model variants.  No effect on non-inout models  (default: 0.5).
Compute device: auto, cpu, cuda, or mps.  'auto' selects CUDA > MPS > CPU  (default: auto).
Reuse the previous gaze result for N frames between inference runs.  0 = no skipping  (default: 0).
Use half-precision (float16) inference on CUDA/MPS (ignored on CPU).
Use torch.compile() for the Gazelle model (PyTorch 2.0+ only).
Enable iris-based gaze refinement (wraps active backend).
Blending weight for iris correction (default: 0.3).
Upscale face crops before iris extraction (default: 2.0).
Path to MGaze model weights (.onnx or .pt)
Architecture name (required for .pt models)
Dataset config key (default: gaze360)
Boost confidence of detections near gaze endpoints.
Multiplicative confidence boost (default: 1.5).
Pixel radius around gaze endpoints (default: 100).
Minimum YOLO conf for sub-threshold candidates (default: 0.10).
Cap on boosted confidence (default: 0.95).
Only boost these class names (default: all non-person classes).
Enable eye movement classification.
Velocity source (default: gaze).
Saccade threshold px/frame (default: 30.0).
Fixation threshold px/frame (default: 10.0).
Min fixation duration frames (default: 4).
Median filter window (default: 3).
Enable novel-salience detection (rapid gaze-shift tracking).
Gaze-endpoint speed threshold in pixels/frame to flag a novel-salience event (default: 40).  Lower = more sensitive.
Minimum frames between consecutive novel-salience events for the same face track (default: 20).
Sliding-window depth for velocity smoothing (default: 2).  1 = instantaneous; 3 = heavier smoothing.
Frames the visual saccade indicator persists after an event (default: 12).
Enable pupillometry tracking.
Measurement mode (default: rgb).
Baseline frames for calibration (default: 90).
Upscale factor for RGB mode (default: 2.0).
IR threshold for dark-pupil segmentation (default: 40).
Ratio smoothing filter (default: kalman).
EMA smoothing alpha -- only used when --pupil-filter is ema (default: 0.3).
Kalman process noise -- controls how fast the filter adapts to ratio changes. Only used when --pupil-filter is kalman (default: 0.0001).
Kalman measurement noise -- higher values smooth more aggressively. Only used when --pupil-filter is kalman (default: 0.01).
EAR threshold for blink detection (default: 0.21).
Min consecutive low-EAR frames for blink (default: 2).
Hampel outlier filter window size (default: 15).
Enable per-eye (left/right) measurements.
